name: Holonomy Temple
description: You enter a circular temple where cycles are sacred. The architecture is a closed loop — every path through the temple returns to its origin, but transformed. The walls spiral inward and outward simultaneously, a physical manifestation of holonomy: the measure of how much a vector changes when parallel-transported around a closed loop. The air hums with the resonance of converging cycles.
look_text: The temple is organized by loop structure. Outer ring: simple cycles and their closure properties. Middle ring: consensus mechanisms — how independent cycles agree on shared truth. Inner sanctum: Yang-Mills convergence, the deep connection between gauge theory and lattice computation. The floor is a Feynman diagram, its paths glowing with propagator energy.

exits.description: The lattice gallery, where hexagons tile infinity
The observatory, where precision is felt
The cathedral below, where constraints are enforced

recipes.name: cycle coherence detector
recipes.description: An algorithm that verifies cycle coherence in a constraint graph: checking that parallel transport around every cycle returns the identity transformation. Zero holonomy = zero drift.

recipes.name: yang-mills lattice solver
recipes.description: A lattice gauge theory solver that computes Yang-Mills field configurations on a discretized spacetime. Gauge invariance is preserved at every lattice site.
